authoritative_sources.title: FAR Part 46 - Quality Assurance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR Part 46 is the primary federal acquisition regulation for government contract quality assurance, inspection, acceptance, and contract quality evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: contract quality obligation and evidence mapping

authoritative_sources.title: FAR 46.407 - Nonconforming supplies or services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAR 46.407 is the primary public rule for handling nonconforming government contract supplies and services, including rejection, acceptance boundaries, technical concurrence, and written evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7007 - Contractor Counterfeit Electronic Part Detection and Avoidance System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This DFARS clause is a primary defense contract obligation for counterfeit electronic part detection, avoidance, supplier flowdown, traceability, reporting, quarantine, and purchasing-system consequences.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier evidence, counterfeit-parts control, and traceability review
used_for: suspect material hold and escalation behavior

authoritative_sources.title: DFARS 252.246-7008 - Sources of Electronic Parts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This DFARS clause defines approved electronic-parts source hierarchy and documentation expectations, directly informing supplier substitution and traceability boundary behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier source hierarchy and substitution control review

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 14 CFR Part 21 is the primary FAA regulatory baseline for aerospace production approvals, production quality systems, conformity, and record obligations for products and articles.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: aerospace production quality-system and release prerequisite review

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A is official public FAA guidance on production-approval quality systems, procedures, supplier controls, production records, conformity, and release-related production controls.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized aerospace 9100-series steward and provides the public aviation, space, and defense QMS vocabulary used for quality, risk, supplier, configuration, and evidence discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: aerospace QMS, contract quality, and control evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG SCMH is a recognized aerospace supply-chain workflow reference for supplier quality, flowdown, product acceptance, special processes, risk, and supply-chain evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: supplier-quality, flowdown, and supply-chain control review
used_for: product acceptance and supplier escalation posture

authoritative_sources.title: Contractor Purchasing System Review Guidebook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCMA's CPSR guidebook is an official defense purchasing-system review reference for procurement-file evidence, subcontractor flowdown, source selection documentation, and supplier-control review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: purchasing-system evidence and supplier flowdown review

workflow_stages.name: intake and source-hierarchy confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm contract number, clause set, prime or subcontract role, customer flowdowns, program, facility, product, lot or serial scope, supplier context, tenant policies, and source hierarchy before making compliance claims.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: obligation and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map FAR, DFARS, FAA, IAQG, customer-flowdown, purchasing-system, counterfeit-parts, supplier, traceability, quality, release, hold, nonconformance, and change-control obligations to controls, evidence records, owners, and review cadence.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: evidence readiness and traceability review
workflow_stages.description: Assess whether ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, CLM, GRC, document-control, production, inventory, lot, serial, supplier, inspection, hold, release, NCR, MRB, and change-control records support the requested compliance posture.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: exception and remediation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route missing evidence, control failures, source conflicts, quality holds, supplier nonconformance, substitution pressure, nonconformance, and operational bypasses to accountable contracts, legal, quality, engineering, supplier, export/security, or executive owners.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: review or attestation preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare internal, customer, DCMA, FAA-support, or supplier review evidence packets without claiming legal signoff, formal certification, quality release, customer acceptance, or regulator acceptance.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: contract clauses, flowdowns, QAPs, CDRLs, program quality plans, source hierarchy records, and policy precedence notes
production orders, travelers, routers, work orders, batch records, lot records, serial histories, as-built/as-maintained records, and change-control records
ERP inventory states, WMS location histories, MES execution records, planning records, and logistics records

supplier master records, approved supplier list status, purchase orders, supplier C of C packages, source inspection artifacts, authorized distributor evidence, procurement files, and supplier corrective-action records
receiving inspection records, first article evidence, inspection acceptance records, quality holds, NCRs, MRB records, concessions, waivers, release records, and rework or repair records
counterfeit-parts screening evidence, suspect part quarantine evidence, disposition records, traceability logs, and reporting or escalation notes
control matrices, evidence checklists, exception registers, remediation trackers, review preparation packets, and owner-routing packages
systems_of_record: export/security control systems where controlled technical data is involved

allowed: classify contract-compliance requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
map named contract, public-source, tenant-policy, supplier, quality, release, and traceability obligations to evidence sources, owners, and review cadence
assess whether visible evidence is present, missing, stale, conflicting, or insufficient for an evidence-backed recommendation
prepare control matrices, evidence readiness summaries, review checklists, exception notes, and remediation escalation packages
route exceptions, missing evidence, policy conflicts, supplier issues, quality holds, and release-boundary concerns to accountable owners
escalate: legal interpretation, regulator-facing action, contracting officer communication, customer negotiation, or formal compliance signoff
quality release, final acceptance, customer acceptance, airworthiness, DER/ODA, engineering disposition, MRB authority, or safety-critical release decision
supplier approval, source substitution, alternate part approval, use-as-is, waiver, concession, repair, rework, or procurement authority
export-control, CUI/security, data-sharing, financial approval, payment withholding, price adjustment, contract modification, or executive exception authority
conflicting public sources, contract clauses, customer flowdowns, tenant policies, quality procedures, or operational practices
refuse: provide legal advice, regulator negotiation, contracting officer decision, formal certification, or final signoff
bypass traceability, release, hold, nonconformance, supplier, purchasing-system, counterfeit-parts, export/security, or quality controls
treat incomplete, stale, contradictory, or non-authoritative records as sufficient proof of compliance
approve supplier substitution, release of held product, MRB disposition, or contract policy override
share or authorize controlled technical data outside approved export/security workflows

domain_failure_modes: contract clause, customer flowdown, tenant policy, and operational practice conflict but the lane gives a confident compliance answer
required supplier C of C, authorized source, lot genealogy, or counterfeit-parts evidence is missing but material is treated as acceptable
ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and supplier records disagree on product identity, lot history, inventory status, or release posture
quality hold, NCR, MRB, concession, waiver, or release prerequisite is bypassed because production schedule pressure is treated as compliance authority
supplier substitution, alternate part, use-as-is, or source change is approved without supplier quality, procurement, engineering, contracts, and customer authority
review preparation drifts into legal interpretation, formal certification, government acceptance prediction, or regulator-facing action
controlled technical data, export-control, or security restrictions are ignored while assembling supplier or compliance evidence
tenant-specific contract clauses or program quality requirements are guessed from public baseline instead of retrieved and escalated

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: contract-specific clause hierarchy and customer flowdowns
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Public sources cannot determine the governing clause hierarchy for a specific prime, subcontract, customer, program, or facility. The pack requires tenant retrieval and escalation when the hierarchy is missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: release, MRB, and supplier approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Authority for release, MRB disposition, supplier substitution, waiver, concession, or customer acceptance is tenant and program specific. The lane can prepare evidence and escalation packages only.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: export, CUI, security, and controlled technical data rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Export/security handling depends on classification, jurisdiction, license, program, supplier location, and tenant controls. The lane refuses authorization and routes to export/security owners.

notes: The source set satisfies the manufacturing-and-supply-chain domain pack by covering public quality standards, supplier and traceability workflow guidance, and release, hold, or nonconformance operating references.

The public baseline constrains workflow terminology and refusal rules. Case-specific posture still requires tenant clauses, customer flowdowns, program procedures, systems-of-record retrieval, and owner authority.
